Plaintiff'S Motion To Strike Defendant Metropolitan Life Insurance Company'S Supplemental Designation Of Oncologist Dr. Van Scoy Mosher As An Expert Witnes And For Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Sep-19-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Attorney, Ryan H. Opgenorth of the law offices of Pillsbury & Coleman LLP (415-433-8000) appears as counsel for plaintiff/moving party

Attorney, James C. Castle of the law offices of Hinshaw & Culbertson LLP (213-680-2800) appears by telephone as counsel for defendant/opposing party

The cause is argued and submitted by both sides as set forth in the transcript of the certified shorthand reporter.

The Court confirms the tentative ruling with modification.

Plaintiff Ma Cristina Mamuyac's motion to strike defendant Metropolitan Life Insurance Company's designation of Dr. Van Scoy-Mosher as an expert witness and request for monetary sanctions is granted in part. Dr. Scoy-Mosher is prohibited from testifying on any subject other than whether Ms. Mamuyac's recurrence of cancer was caused in whole or in part by her anxiety and depression and only if Ms. Mamuyac presents expert evidence in her case in chief on that subject. Because MetLife's supplemental designation of Dr. Scoy-Mosher did not accurately reflect MetLife's position taken in its opposition papers as to the scope of the intended testimony of Dr. Scoy-Mosher, that designation lacked substantial justification and warrants an award of sanctions in favor of Ms. Mamuyac of $5000 payable by October 3, 2017, the costs she reasonably incurred in bringing this motion. Ms. Mamuyac is not precluded by anything in this order from filing a motion in limine with the trial judge regarding the proposed testimony of Dr. Scoy-Mosher. Written order is signed and filed in open court.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: J. Rios Merida; Reporter: Gena Eales, CSR No: 9176, email: geales@sftc.org. (D302)

RULING - A R G U E D. The Court inquires re: financial records from 2010-2012. The Court adopts the tenative ruling with modifications as follows:

DEFENDANT METROPOLITAN LIFE INSURANCE COMPANY's Motion To Compel Further Responses To Request For Production, Set One. Defendant Metropolitan Life Insurance Company's motion to compel further responses to set one document requests is denied. The Court Denies the request for financial records prior to 2013. Based on the report of the judge pro tem and the separate statement filed by MetLife, it appears that the remaining issue is whether plaintiff Ma Mamuyac should be required to produce financial information for the years 2010-2012. MetLife has not shown that Ms. Mamuyac's financial information for those years is directly relevant to any of Ms. Mamuyac's claims or MetLife's defenses. Plaintiff to prepare a proposed order which contains all of the rulings including the agreement of parties to produce bank records from 2013-2016 and tax records for 2016 in compliance with CRC 3.1312.
